group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.1 released; coordinated Merlin/LSP class hover improvements; js_of_ocaml Wasm fixes; opam shell/UI PRs in review.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune adds DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT env var, relocatable compiler support, Windows hardlink sandboxing, and multiple (dirs ..) stanzas.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable** ([ocaml/dune#13934](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13934)): Actions can now observe their project root via `DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT`, avoiding manual `%{project_root}` threading — merged by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Relocatable compiler support in package management** ([ocaml/dune#13321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13321)): Dune detects relocatable compilers (via `relocatable-compiler` meta-package or OCaml 5.5+), enabling the compiler to be cached like a regular package — merged by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Hardlink sandboxing on Windows** ([ocaml/dune#13987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13987)): Restriction on hardlink sandboxing on Windows lifted; symlink attempts now produce a clearer error — merged by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), partially addressing [ocaml/dune#4362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4362)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — `c_library_flags` in `foreign_library`** ([ocaml/dune#13484](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13484)): Flags can now be passed to `ocamlmklib` via `c_library_flags` in `foreign_library` stanzas — merged by [@madroach](https://github.com/madroach)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Multiple `(dirs ..)` stanzas** ([ocaml/dune#13734](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13734)): Support for multiple `(dirs ..)` stanzas with intersection semantics from dune language 3.23, fixing [ocaml/dune#6249](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/6249) — merged by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Improved `diff` action inference** ([ocaml/dune#14008](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14008)): Source directories are now automatically inferred as dependencies when used as the left-hand side in `diff` actions — merged by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) — Type enclosing for class types and objects** ([ocaml/merlin#2053](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2053)): New `Classtype` variant in `type_enclosing.mli` with expanded logic for class types, class type aliases, and object expressions, addressing [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587) — merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) — Hover support for class and object constructs** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1599)): Hovering over class declarations, class fields, object expressions, and record brackets now triggers type-enclosing queries, addressing [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587) — merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) — Reference unboxing substitution fix** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2210](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2210)): Fixed a substitution bug in `js_assign.ml` that caused assertion failures when compiling containers-data 3.18 — merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) — Wasm physical identity of empty closures** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207)): Function equality semantics are now correctly preserved in the Wasm backend — merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) — Wasm stale global data flow guard** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208)): Guard added to prevent crashes on certain direct function calls in the Wasm backend — merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Six of nine repositories had activity this week; [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind), [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format), and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no recorded commits or PRs
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) was the most active repository with the 3.22.1 patch release ([ocaml/dune#13947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13947)), six new merged features, and extensive test/CI work
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) executed a coordinated improvement to class and object hover support, both driven by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and addressing [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587)
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged three targeted bug fixes for the Wasm backend and reference unboxing, with large draft PRs for OCaml 5.5 and ESM support in progress
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) had three PRs under active review: Nushell shell support ([ocaml/opam#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873)), action list reordering ([ocaml/opam#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864)), and a symlink fix ([ocaml/opam#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6874))
- [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) received two new PRs: OxCaml unboxed type support ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)) and a long-requested `@inline` tag for module declarations ([ocaml/odoc#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408))
- Release preparation is underway for both Merlin 5.7.0-504 ([ocaml/merlin#2055](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2055)) and ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-504 ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Active week of bug fixes, ongoing indexer performance work, opam PR reviews, and release preparation across multiple repositories.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Dune 3.22.1 released** ([ocaml/dune#13947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13947)): Patch release by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) fixing Windows 7 compatibility, `dune test` crash without default context ([ocaml/dune#13930](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13930), [ocaml/dune#13904](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13904)), and Chrome trace format timestamps ([ocaml/dune#13911](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13911))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Digest caching correctness fixes**: Cache now invalidates on device/inode numbers rather than floating-point mtime ([ocaml/dune#13991](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13991)) with a follow-up inode field correction ([ocaml/dune#14058](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14058)) — by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Sandbox fix for `(:include ..)` deps** ([ocaml/dune#13898](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13898)): Sandbox specifications inside `(:include foo.sexp)` expressions were previously ignored — fixed by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — macOS SIGCHLD crash fix** ([ocaml/dune#14047](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14047)): Ignores unset SIGCHLD signals during SIGINT cleanup on macOS — merged by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — RPC error message improvement** ([ocaml/dune#13983](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13983)): Error now reads "Another Dune instance is currently running. Aborting..." — contributed by [Meet Zalavadiya](https://github.com/meet0806), closing [ocaml/dune#13902](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13902)

- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Backporting script** ([ocaml/dune#13952](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13952)): Automates cherry-picking fixes to release candidate branches — merged by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Marshal-to-repr digest migration in progress**: Multi-PR effort ([ocaml/dune#14015](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14015), [ocaml/dune#14016](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14016), [ocaml/dune#14025](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14025)) replacing `marshal`-based digests with `repr`-derived functions; culminating open PR [ocaml/dune#14051](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14051) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Per-module library dependency analysis** ([ocaml/dune#14021](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14021), [ocaml/dune#14030](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14030)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) implementing finer dependency filtering using `ocamldep` to reduce recompilation, tracking [ocaml/dune#4572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4572)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Rocq updated to 9.2** ([ocaml/dune#13996](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13996)): Drops OCaml 4 from Rocq support — merged by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) — Pointing indexes for `ocaml-index`** under review ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d)'s PR adds index files that reference another on-disk index, yielding ~2.7x write performance improvement
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) — LRU cache for indexer memory** under review ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)): [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) replaces an unbounded `Hashtable` with an LRU cache to bound memory consumption
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) — Forward-compatibility fix for `keep-expansion`** ([ocaml/merlin#2054](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2054)): Type annotation added in `Destruct.refine_complete_match` to avoid a type scope escape that will become a hard error in a future OCaml version — by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) — Signature-help readability revert** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1598)): Line-break formatting from [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580) reverted because editors render signature-help labels as one-liners — merged by [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo)

- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) — Wasm Dynlink/toplevel support** in progress ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)): Large in-progress PR by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) (6000+ lines)

group_summaries.cross_repository_work:

group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Concurrent dune invocations and RPC locking** ([ocaml/dune#13964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13964)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) debated retry-on-lock-contention vs. immediate failure; AI agent workflows cited as a key motivating use case, with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) arguing immediate failure is preferable for agent loops
- **Deprecating `(mode promote)`** ([ocaml/dune#14044](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14044)): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed deprecating promote rule mode, arguing both use cases are better served by `diff` actions and a planned `--promote-file` flag
- **`scope` stanza for library visibility when vendoring** ([ocaml/dune#13337](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13337)): [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht) proposing `(scope (dir ..) (packages ..))` to control library visibility; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) raised concerns about long-term maintenance commitment before accepting new dune language features
- **Finer library dependency analysis** ([ocaml/dune#14021](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14021)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) implementing per-module dependency filtering with active review from [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) on prerequisite PRs ([ocaml/dune#14017](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14017), [ocaml/dune#14030](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14030), [ocaml/dune#14031](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14031))
- **Pointing index race condition** ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) identified that using `with_open` to check a file identifier before re-opening provides no safety guarantee if the file is replaced between the two opens; recommendation is to check an already-open file descriptor
- **LRU cache correctness in indexer** ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) flagged that removing a cache entry when an offset is no longer needed loses sharing, causing duplicate allocations; benchmarks show ~1.1x memory penalty
- **`-short-paths` configuration in ocaml-lsp** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1579)): [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) clarified that `-real-paths` is Merlin-specific and cannot be passed directly; discussion settled on restoring upstream defaults with a Dune workaround for users needing explicit control
- **macOS SIGCHLD signal handling** ([ocaml/dune#14023](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14023)): [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) investigated unreliable signal delivery during high-concurrency builds; mitigation merged ([ocaml/dune#14047](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14047)) while a broader C-stub self-pipe fix remains in draft
- **DockerHub opam versioning** ([ocaml/opam#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875)): [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) raised confusion over images shipping `opam 2.0.10`; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) explained that `/usr/bin/opam` targets the lowest supported release for test coverage breadth, while `/usr/bin/opam-<version>` provides the latest; DockerHub description updated and issue closed
- **odoc `@inline` tag design** ([ocaml/odoc#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564)): Long-standing 2021 issue on inlining submodule expansions into the parent page revisited by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) with concrete implementation [ocaml/odoc#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408), reviving discussion on inline-by-default vs. opt-in behaviour
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml ecosystem compatibility**: OxCaml-related work appeared in both [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#13981](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13981)) and [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)), with [ocaml/merlin#2054](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2054) also ensuring forward compatibility with upcoming OCaml compiler changes, indicating a coordinated push across the toolchain
- **Windows parity in dune**: Hardlink sandboxing enabled ([ocaml/dune#13987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13987)), line-ending CI check added ([ocaml/dune#14013](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14013)), and Windows path-rewriting crash reproduction test added ([ocaml/dune#13988](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13988)); [ocaml/dune#11161](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11161) continues tracking the full Windows package management story
- **Marshal-to-repr digest migration**: Multi-PR effort in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#14015](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14015), [ocaml/dune#14016](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14016), [ocaml/dune#14025](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14025)) replacing generic `marshal`-based digest functions with type-safe `repr`-derived ones; culminating PR [ocaml/dune#14051](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14051) claims improved boot speed
- **Merlin indexer scalability**: Two concurrent PRs ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050), [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) target `ocaml-index` from complementary directions — bounded memory via LRU eviction and reduced write volume via pointer-based index sharing — signaling a push to make the indexer viable for larger codebases
- **Wasm backend advancement in js_of_ocaml**: Three correctness fixes merged this week ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2210](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2210)) alongside large in-progress PRs for Dynlink/toplevel ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) and OCaml 5.5 support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195))
- **Relocatable compiler integration in dune**: Following the merge of [ocaml/dune#13321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13321), issue [ocaml/dune#14012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14012) outlines the next step of preferring the relocatable compiler by default, reducing the need for toolchain rebuilds
- **Release process automation in dune**: The backport script ([ocaml/dune#13952](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13952)), reusable revdep workflow ([ocaml/dune#14014](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14014)), and multi-target revdep fix ([ocaml/dune#14024](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14024)) reflect sustained investment in reducing manual release overhead

group_summaries.brief_summary: 47 PRs merged in oxcaml/oxcaml on staging/GADTs, new modes, static modules, and backend optimizations; Merlin synced with upstream.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Abstract kinds, write/corrupted modes, static modules, staging/GADTs soundness, X86 peepholes, IRC threshold, and new Lfor strategy.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Abstract kinds `with kind_` constraints** completed the core abstract kinds feature set via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629), in advance of a formal announcement.
- **New `write` and `writing` modes** were added in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608), and **`corrupted` and `corruptible` modes** (the contention/portability analogues) followed in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713), enabling cleaner mode implications without needing `shared`.
- **Static modules** landed via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5724](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5724) and the underlying static computation framework via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5430).
- **Staging and GADTs** became mostly sound together through the combined parts 1-3 PR [oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641), with stage tracking in `Ctype` across quotes/splices and exhaustive GADT tests.
- **Quotes of expansive expressions** are now typed at mode `once` in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698), and top-level `Pstr_eval` expressions can be at mode `once` via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5715](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5715).
- **Sound moding of quotes and splices** with tests was merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697).
- **Type checking `let poly_`** landed in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5513](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5513), with a companion PR adding `val poly_` support opened as [oxcaml/oxcaml#5755](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5755).
- **jkind annotation errors** in quotes are now properly raised rather than silently dropped, with assertions added in `Translquote` via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674).
- **X86 peephole optimizations** infrastructure was merged in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639), adding rules for dead-register removal and redundant comparison elimination.
- **New `Lfor` compilation strategy** was introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5728](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5728).
- **IRC complexity threshold** for register allocation was added in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5705](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5705), allowing fallback to a simpler allocator for large functions.
- **`-thunkify-compilation-unit-initialization`** flag added for wasm_of_ocaml support in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5720](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5720).
- **`Atomic.make_contended`** was made to work on runtime4 in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5640](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5640).
- **GC stat samples** now record current minor words in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5030).
- **Metaprogramming type support** in Merlin's `locate_types.ml`: `Tquote_eval` is now represented via a `Type_ref` node pointing to `Predef.path_eval`, and `Tquote` recursively builds a type tree for staged expressions ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228), [oxcaml/merlin#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230)).
- **New mode documentation** in Merlin for `Write` visibility and `Writing` statefulness modes, plus `Observing` renamed to `Reading`, contributed by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228)).
- **New ignored compiler flags** in Merlin's `mconfig.ml`: `-requires-metaprogramming`, `-uses-metaprogramming`, and five x86 peephole flags, preventing rejection of projects using upstream compiler options ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228)).
- **Bug fix**: allocation size for recursive mixed blocks on bytecode corrected in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5754](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5754), preventing segfaults with void fields.
- **Bug fix**: tricky parallel Memprof bugs fixed in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5600](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5600).
- **Bug fix**: `moregeneral` now correctly regeneralizes layout vars on error via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5691](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5691).
- **Performance**: `caml_modify` calls reduced when writing parameterized unboxed records in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5736](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5736).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) had the most activity with 47 merged PRs spanning language features (staging/GADTs, modes, static modules, abstract kinds), Flambda2/reaper improvements, backend codegen optimizations, and the ongoing OCaml 5.4 merge track.
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) merged a large upstream sync from oxcaml/oxcaml@5.2.0minus-35 ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228), ~27k additions across 131 files), with follow-up fixes for metaprogramming type handling ([oxcaml/merlin#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230)) and an OCaml 5.4 upgrade PR opened ([oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)).
- [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) received one PR from [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) to update the bootstrap compiler to OCaml 5.4.1 ([oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36)), keeping opam switch creation tooling current.
- [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml) and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity this week.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Active week in oxcaml/oxcaml with Flambda2 reaper cleanup, CFG pass additions, n-way join work, and 5.4 merge track PRs alongside Merlin's upstream sync.
group_summaries.activity:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774) proposes representing all-value/void **mixed records and variants as uniform blocks**, bumping mixed block version to v6 and removing the 254-value limit.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738) proposes **improved codegen for int comparisons** using XOR before comparison and a better 3-way comparison lowering.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5746](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5746) proposes **eliminating useless shifts in string/bytes indexed access**.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5740](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5740) proposes **duplicating comparisons in CFG block terminators** for select/cmov.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5742](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5742) opens work on **unwinding support via sframes**.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5751](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5751) continues the n-way join work adding **extra params for CSE-like variables at join points**, a reloaded version of [oxcaml/oxcaml#5733](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5733).
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5753](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5753) uses **constant propagation to rewrite CFG operations**.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5743](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5743) adds a CFG pass to **move instructions common to several blocks**.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5761](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5761) proposes **flipping the default for `SPLIT_AROUND_LOOPS`** regalloc parameter, and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5762](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5762) proposes **enabling prologue shrink-wrap by default**.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5734](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5734) adds initial **aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu support in the dissector**; related tracking issue [oxcaml/oxcaml#5732](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5732) opened for arm64 Linux.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5747](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5747) fixes a **bug involving effects, callbacks and locals**.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5771](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5771) fixes a **bug in meet for array element kinds** in Flambda2.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770) proposes using **callback modules to emulate function specialisation** in the patricia tree, targeting a ~1% compiler speedup.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5772](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5772) adds **most missing primitives to fexpr** to support full compiler/stdlib IR dumps.
- Reaper cleanups merged: [oxcaml/oxcaml#5764](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5764) (more intuitive `-dreaper` flag), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5744](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5744) (remove `{co,}field_{usages,sources}`), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5759](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5759) (add profile calls in solver), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5731](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5731) (dep_solver reorganization).
- The **5.4 merge track** advanced with [oxcaml/oxcaml#5765](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5765) (dynlink build tidy-up), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717) (make install progress), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711) (testsuite conflict resolution), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5729](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5729) (5.4 merge miscellany), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5694](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5694) (disable alerts in testsuite), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5534](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5534) (toplevel conflict resolution).
- [oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227) (opened by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)) upgrades Merlin to OCaml 5.4, requiring a move to ocamlformat 0.29.0 and touching 62 files.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin upstream sync** [oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) imported oxcaml/oxcaml@5.2.0minus-35 changes, including the metaprogramming/staging system (`Tquote`, `Tquote_eval`, `Tsplice`), layout polymorphism (`Tpat_fun_layout`), and mode system extensions — requiring Merlin-specific conflict resolution in `typecore.ml` and `ctype.ml` by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923).
- **X86 peephole flags** merged in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639) (five new compiler flags) required corresponding additions to Merlin's ignored-flags allowlist in [oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) to prevent Merlin from rejecting projects using these flags.
- **Metaprogramming compiler flags** `-requires-metaprogramming` and `-uses-metaprogramming` from oxcaml/oxcaml required allowlisting in Merlin's `mconfig.ml` as part of [oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228).
- [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) drove staging/metaprogramming work in oxcaml/oxcaml ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698)) and also reviewed the Merlin upstream sync [oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228), identifying and fixing the `Tquote_eval` representation bug resolved in [oxcaml/merlin#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230).
- [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) contributed the `corrupted`/`corruptible` modes to oxcaml/oxcaml in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713) and also contributed mode documentation entries for `Write` visibility and `Writing` statefulness to Merlin in [oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228).
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) drove the OxCaml 5.4 merge track in oxcaml/oxcaml ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5765](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5765), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711)) and was tagged as reviewer for [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) to keep bootstrap compiler packaging aligned.
- **Bootstrap compiler tracking**: [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) (opened by [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou)) updates the `ocaml-variants` opam file to OCaml 5.4.1, following the progression of the 5.4 merge track in oxcaml/oxcaml and ensuring `opam-compiler` switch creation works reliably.
- The **`-thunkify-compilation-unit-initialization`** flag added in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5720](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5720) targets wasm_of_ocaml, the WebAssembly sibling of the js_of_ocaml toolchain in [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml).
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627) (**Optimized Patricia trees**): detailed debate between [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) on specialising `Set`/`Map` vs. fixing compiler function specialisation, with benchmarks showing a ~5.7% full-repo speedup. [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770) as an alternative using callback modules.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5733](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5733) / [oxcaml/oxcaml#5751](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5751) (**n-way join extra params**): discussion between [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) on structuring the boundary between join analysis and join code generation, leading to the reloaded version with improved separation.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641) (**GADTs with staging**): detailed review from [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) covering closure allocations, exception handling in environment traversals, and the fragility of stage tracking in error messages; a ticket was created to track accurate stage information in error traces.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#3181](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3181) (**Missed CSE opportunity**): updated with a new reproducer found during investigation of [oxcaml/oxcaml#5691](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5691), noting that [oxcaml/oxcaml#5733](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5733) addresses it via the n-way join.
- [oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) (**Upstream sync review**): [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) identified that the `Tquote_eval` handling in `ptyp_of_type` was incorrect and provided the correct representation, promptly addressed in [oxcaml/merlin#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230); the PR also called out that `Tquote_eval` and `Tpat_fun_layout` changes warrant expert review from metaprogramming and layout polymorphism specialists.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Staging and metaprogramming system maturing rapidly**: GADTs, local constraints, sound moding, and jkind assertions all landed in a single week ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674)), forming a coherent prototype; `Tquote`/`Tquote_eval` type forms are now appearing across multiple analysis modules in Merlin ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228)).
- **Modes system expansion**: `write`/`writing` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)) and `corrupted`/`corruptible` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)) modes fill gaps in mode implications; a full mode polymorphism system is in draft in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5478](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5478); mode documentation is being extended in Merlin in parallel ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228)).
- **Backend codegen improvements accumulating**: X86 peephole optimizations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), improved int comparison lowering ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738)), CFG duplicate comparisons ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5740](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5740)), prologue shrink-wrap ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5762](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5762)), and constant propagation in CFG ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5753](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5753)) are all in flight concurrently.
- **Flambda2 reaper systematic cleanup**: architectural reorganization removing redundant state, adding profiling, and restructuring rules ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5764](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5764), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5744](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5744), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5759](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5759), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5731](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5731)) in preparation for broader enablement.
- **Merlin upstream sync maintenance burden**: each oxcaml/oxcaml release requires Merlin to handle new type constructors (quote/splice, layout patterns) and add new compiler flags to its allowlist ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228)); an OCaml 5.4 upgrade is also needed ([oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)).
- **5.4 merge track advancing steadily**: multiple conflict-resolution and testsuite promotion PRs land each week in oxcaml/oxcaml, with the bootstrap compiler version in oxcaml/opam-repository being updated in parallel ([oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36)) to keep opam tooling current.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Bytecode fixes, stdlib additions, and a Pphyscomp optimisation landed in ocaml/ocaml; ocaml/RFCs revived the implicit source positions design debate.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dynarray gained iarray conversion, Pphyscomp lambda primitive landed, and record update syntax was relaxed for application expressions.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [Dynarray.of_iarray and Dynarray.to_iarray](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14693) added to the standard library, enabling O(n) round-trips between dynamic and immutable arrays.
- [ocaml/ocaml#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13588) merged after a long review: physical comparison operators (`==` and `!=`) now propagate as a distinct `Pphyscomp` primitive to the lambda IR, enabling backends such as js_of_ocaml to emit semantically correct code for these operators.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14692) relaxed record update syntax so that `{ (Gc.get ()) with ... }` no longer requires surrounding parentheses around function application expressions.
- s390x CFI (Call Frame Information) debug metadata improved via [ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500), adding a `cfi_val_offset` emission helper and GDB regression tests.
- The `PUSHENVACC4` bytecode opcode, silently never emitted due to an off-by-one in the peephole optimiser, is now correctly generated following [ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) had a productive week: five PRs merged covering stdlib additions ([ocaml/ocaml#14693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14693)), bytecode correctness ([ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534), [ocaml/ocaml#14641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14641)), lambda optimisation ([ocaml/ocaml#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13588)), and parser relaxation ([ocaml/ocaml#14692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14692)).
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) saw renewed activity on [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) (implicit source positions) after approximately a year of inactivity, with a proposal to resolve the stalled design debate via a caml-devel meeting.
- A CVE (CVE-2026-34353) was assigned for the `Bigarray.reshape` overflow reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655).
- Several new PRs were opened addressing libunix thread-safety ([ocaml/ocaml#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715)), typing error hints ([ocaml/ocaml#14695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14695)), and ocamltest infrastructure ([ocaml/ocaml#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: New PRs opened for libunix thread-safety, typing hints, ocamltest infra, and package type syntax; Merlin typing recovery upstreaming saw active review.
group_summaries.activity:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715) opened by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) to fix NULL-dereference bugs in POSIX thread-safe libunix functions (`getgrnam_r` and related wrappers), with a cherry-pick request for OCaml 5.5.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposes a new `cold` ocamltest action for tests disabled on CI by default but still run on developer machines.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14710) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) extends package type syntax to accept `with type t = int with type x = float` as a synonym for `with type t = int and type x = float`.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14712](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14712) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) proposes an all-in-one expect test runner executing normal, `-principal`, and `-rectypes` checks in a single process with state reset.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14707) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) enables REPL tracing of module-dependent functions (`#trace show;;` now works for modular explicit arguments).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14709](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14709) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) proposes changing ocamldoc HTML page titles from `Library name : Module_name` to `Module_name (Library name)`.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14703) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) fixes swapped `None`/`Some` branches in `dll.ml` when upgrading a DLL entry from Checking to Execution mode.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14702) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged: fixes a `Load_path.add` bug where files from a second hidden directory with overlapping basenames could leak into the visible files table.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14695) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) adds an actionable hint to the GADT scope-escape error message pointing users toward type annotations and the manual.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14642) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) merged: the runtime now silently skips `caml_shutdown` cleanup when unjoined domains remain rather than segfaulting; superseded PR [ocaml/ocaml#14614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14614) was closed.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14641) moved `opnames.ml.c` and `opnames.mli` from `tools/` into `bytecomp/` to resolve a dependency ordering issue.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14690) by [cui](https://github.com/cuiweixie) fixed an ambiguous type path expansion bug in error trace reporting.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241) by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) (Merlin typing recovery upstreaming) received an active review round, with [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) approving the overall structure.
- [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) (implicit source positions) saw renewed discussion after approximately a year of silence, with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) summarizing outstanding design questions and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) proposing a caml-devel meeting to break the deadlock.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) is active in both repositories: as release manager merging PRs in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) and as a key participant re-energising the implicit source positions RFC in [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52).
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) connected work across both repositories: responding to the ppxlib extension point request in [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) and simultaneously proposing to escalate [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) to the caml-devel meeting, suggesting these language design discussions benefit from the same structured meeting format.
- The ppxlib AST stability request in [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) (adding extension point entries to ease frozen-AST strategies) mirrors the broader pattern of features awaiting compiler-level support that the RFCs process is intended to formalise.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580) on GC for continuations saw a new proposal: rather than `discontinue`-ing unreachable continuations from finalisers, a first step could free fiber stack memory only, with an optional explicit `Gc.flush()` API for users needing finalisation at a known point.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) requesting new extension point entries in the OCaml AST to aid ppxlib's frozen-AST strategy received a constructive response from [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), who suggested opening a concrete PR ahead of an upcoming developer meeting.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676) on preventing shell injection in GitHub Actions workflows is stalled: environment variable names conflict with GitHub's own reserved names (e.g., `GITHUB_REF_NAME`), complicating the security fix.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) on parallel test segfaults in cleanup-at-exit mode continued: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) traced a race condition in `ephetest_par` where domains can deadlock waiting on ephemerons, reproduced reliably by enabling GC verbose logging.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655) on overflow in `Bigarray.reshape` now has CVE-2026-34353 assigned.
- [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) implicit source positions: the unresolved design question is whether call-site positions should use `Lexing.position` (option 1) or a richer location type (option 2b); [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) argues for 2b with option 1 only if performance concerns are demonstrated.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- The runtime team is working through several multicore-specific correctness issues concurrently: continuation memory leaks ([ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580)), cleanup-at-exit races ([ocaml/ocaml#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14642)), and ephemeron GC liveness ([ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594)).
- Multiple bytecode-related fixes landed in the same week ([ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534), [ocaml/ocaml#14641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14641), [ocaml/ocaml#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13588)), reflecting increased attention to the bytecode backend's correctness and its use as a compilation target by tools such as js_of_ocaml.
- The ppxlib ecosystem is pushing for upstream changes to ease long-term AST stability, with [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) opening a formal discussion track.
- Long-stalled RFCs with prior design consensus are being revived via structured meetings rather than async PR discussion, as seen with [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) after approximately a year of inactivity.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Activity confined to eio: configurable eventfd/pipe backend parameters added; two open issues received discussion.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: eio gains optional eventfd/pipe parameters in Linux and Posix backends for shell and tool authors.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Configurable eventfd creation** in the [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) Linux backend: `run_event_loop` and `run` in `lib_eio_linux` now accept an optional `?eventfd` parameter, allowing callers to supply a custom function for creating event file descriptors ([ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836))
- **Configurable pipe creation** in the Posix backend: `lib_eio_posix` domain manager and scheduler now accept an optional `?pipe` parameter, enabling callers to control how pipes are created — useful for shells that need to move FDs to higher numbers (e.g. `dash savefd` pattern) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was limited to [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio); all other repositories (domainslib, ocaml-uring, meio, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) had no recorded commits or discussions
- One new PR was opened in eio ([ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836)) making Linux and Posix backend event FD creation configurable, affecting 8 files (35 additions, 26 deletions)
- Two pre-existing issues received follow-up discussion: [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) (Path.load race condition) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) (lock-free waiters)
- Contributors this week: [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Patrick Ferris opened eio#836 to make backend event FDs configurable; two longstanding issues received follow-up comments.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) **Make Linux and Posix eventfd functions configurable**, currently unmerged, to support shells like `msh` that need to relocate FDs to higher numbers
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) commented on [ocaml-multicore/eio#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) **Make Waiters lock-free**, noting that part of the referenced algorithm is already in use via `cells.mli`
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) filed [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) documenting silent truncation in `Eio.Path.load` on race conditions and empty results for `/proc` virtual files; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) noted overlap with [ocaml-multicore/eio#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798) and proposed splitting the concerns into separate issues
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) reported in [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) that `Eio.Path.load` uses file size as an exact buffer bound rather than a hint, causing silent truncation under race conditions and returning empty strings for `/proc` virtual files; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) acknowledged overlap with [ocaml-multicore/eio#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798) and suggested splitting the race condition concern into a dedicated issue
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted on [ocaml-multicore/eio#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) that the `cells.mli` module already implements part of the lock-free waiter design described in the referenced Kotlin coroutines paper, updating the status of that long-running design discussion
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Backend extensibility for system-level tools**: [ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) reflects a pattern where shell authors and low-level tool writers need hooks into how Eio creates internal file descriptors, pointing toward a broader need for configurable backend primitives
- **`Eio.Path.load` correctness for virtual filesystems**: both [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798) highlight that `Path.load`'s stat-then-read approach is unsuitable for virtual filesystems (e.g. `/proc`) and concurrent environments, suggesting a fix is becoming a priority

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.1 released; OxCaml merged 47 PRs on staging/GADTs, new modes, static modules; ocaml/ocaml landed 5 PRs with Dynarray conversions and Pphyscomp.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Dune gains DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT and relocatable compiler support; OxCaml lands abstract kinds, write/corrupted modes, static modules; Dynarray.of_iarray/to_iarray added to stdlib.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Dynarray.of_iarray and Dynarray.to_iarray](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14693) added to the standard library, enabling O(n) round-trips between dynamic and immutable arrays.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Physical comparison operators** (`==` and `!=`) now propagate as a distinct `Pphyscomp` primitive to the lambda IR via [ocaml/ocaml#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13588), enabling backends such as js_of_ocaml to emit semantically correct code.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Record update syntax** relaxed so that `{ (Gc.get ()) with ... }` no longer requires parentheses around function application expressions via [ocaml/ocaml#14692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14692).
- __RUMINANT:core__ **s390x CFI debug metadata** improved via [ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500), adding a `cfi_val_offset` emission helper and GDB regression tests.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`PUSHENVACC4` bytecode opcode** now correctly generated after an off-by-one fix in the peephole optimiser via [ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Abstract kinds `with kind_` constraints** completed the core abstract kinds feature set via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New `write`/`writing` modes** added in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) and **`corrupted`/`corruptible` modes** in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713), enabling cleaner mode implications without needing `shared`.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Static modules** landed via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5724](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5724) and the underlying static computation framework via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5430).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Staging and GADTs** became mostly sound together through parts 1-3 in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641), with stage tracking in `Ctype` and exhaustive GADT tests.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **X86 peephole optimizations** infrastructure merged in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639), adding rules for dead-register removal and redundant comparison elimination.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **IRC complexity threshold** for register allocation added in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5705](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5705), allowing fallback to a simpler allocator for large functions.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New `Lfor` compilation strategy** introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5728](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5728).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`-thunkify-compilation-unit-initialization`** flag added for wasm_of_ocaml support in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5720](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5720).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Metaprogramming type support** in Merlin's `locate_types.ml` with `Tquote_eval` and `Tquote` type forms via [oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) and [oxcaml/merlin#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230).
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Configurable eventfd creation** in the [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) Linux and Posix backends, with optional `?eventfd` and `?pipe` parameters via [ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Rocq 9.2.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29617) merged to opam-repository by [Guillaume Melquiond](https://github.com/silene), adding rocq-runtime, rocq-stdlib, rocq-devtools, and rocqide packages.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **opentelemetry 0.90** [ocaml/opam-repository#29633](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29633) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube), a major refactor splitting signal providers into separate traces, metrics, and logs packages.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **sm 0.1.0** [ocaml/opam-repository#29641](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29641) — a pure OCaml implementation of Chinese national cryptographic algorithms (SM2, SM3, SM4, ZUC).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **aslref 1.0-EAC1** [ocaml/opam-repository#29635](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29635) — ARM's reference implementation of the Architecture Specification Language (ASL).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **lunar 1.0.0** [ocaml/opam-repository#29625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29625) by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw), a date management library with Time, Date, Datetime, Timezone, and Zoned_datetime modules.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune `DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT` environment variable** [ocaml/dune#13934](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13934) allows actions to observe their project root without manual `%{project_root}` threading.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Relocatable compiler support** in dune package management [ocaml/dune#13321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13321) enables the compiler to be cached like a regular package.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Hardlink sandboxing on Windows** [ocaml/dune#13987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13987) lifts the prior restriction and provides clearer errors on symlink failures.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Multiple `(dirs ..)` stanzas** with intersection semantics [ocaml/dune#13734](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13734) from dune language 3.23.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Class and object hover support** coordinated across [ocaml/merlin#2053](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2053) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1599) by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), addressing [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml Wasm correctness fixes**: reference unboxing [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2210](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2210), empty closure physical identity [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207), and stale global data flow guard [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208) all merged.

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Flambda2 reaper cleanups, 5.4 merge track PRs, dune digest migration, Merlin indexer scalability work, and opam Nushell/action-sort PRs under review.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715) opened by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) to fix NULL-dereference bugs in POSIX thread-safe libunix functions, with a cherry-pick request for OCaml 5.5.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposes a new `cold` ocamltest action for tests disabled on CI by default.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14710) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) extends package type syntax to accept `with type t = int with type x = float` as a synonym for `and`.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14707) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) enables REPL tracing of module-dependent functions.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241) by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) (Merlin typing recovery upstreaming) received an active review round with [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) approving the overall structure.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) (implicit source positions) saw renewed discussion after approximately a year of silence, with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) summarizing outstanding design questions and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) proposing a caml-devel meeting.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ Flambda2 reaper systematic cleanups merged: [oxcaml/oxcaml#5764](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5764) (more intuitive `-dreaper` flag), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5744](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5744) (remove `{co,}field_{usages,sources}`), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5759](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5759) (add profile calls in solver), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5731](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5731) (dep_solver reorganization).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **5.4 merge track** advanced with [oxcaml/oxcaml#5765](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5765) (dynlink build tidy-up), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717) (make install progress), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711) (testsuite conflict resolution), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5729](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5729) (5.4 merge miscellany), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5534](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5534) (toplevel conflict resolution).

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Concurrent dune invocations and RPC locking** ([ocaml/dune#13964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13964)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) debated retry-on-lock-contention vs. immediate failure; AI agent workflows were cited as a key motivating use case, with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) arguing immediate failure is preferable for agent loops.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Optimized Patricia trees** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627)): detailed debate between [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) on specialising `Set`/`Map` vs. fixing compiler function specialisation, with benchmarks showing a ~5.7% full-repo speedup; [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770) as an alternative using callback modules.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **GADTs with staging review** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641)): detailed review from [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) covering closure allocations, exception handling in environment traversals, and fragility of stage tracking in error messages; a ticket was created to track accurate stage information in error traces.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **GC for continuations** ([ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580)): new proposal to free fiber stack memory only as a first step rather than `discontinue`-ing unreachable continuations from finalisers, with an optional explicit `Gc.flush()` API for users needing finalisation at a known point.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **ppxlib AST stability** ([ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668)): [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) requested new extension point entries to aid ppxlib's frozen-AST strategy; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) suggested opening a concrete PR ahead of an upcoming developer meeting.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **CVE-2026-34353 assigned** for overflow in `Bigarray.reshape` [ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655), now with a formal CVE identifier.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin pointing index race condition** ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) identified that using `with_open` to check a file identifier before re-opening provides no safety guarantee if the file is replaced between the two opens.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Deprecating `(mode promote)`** ([ocaml/dune#14044](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14044)): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed deprecating promote rule mode, arguing both use cases are better served by `diff` actions and a planned `--promote-file` flag.
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Eio.Path.load` correctness for virtual filesystems**: [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) reported in [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) that `Path.load` uses file size as an exact buffer bound causing silent truncation and empty results for `/proc` files; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) acknowledged overlap with [ocaml-multicore/eio#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798).

-**CVE-2026-34353 assigned** for overflow in `Bigarray.reshape` [ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655), now with a formal CVE identifier.
tools: -**Concurrent dune invocations and RPC locking** ([ocaml/dune#13964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13964)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) debated retry-on-lock-contention vs. immediate failure; AI agent workflows were cited as a key motivating use case, with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) arguing immediate failure is preferable for agent loops.

-**Merlin pointing index race condition** ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) identified that using `with_open` to check a file identifier before re-opening provides no safety guarantee if the file is replaced between the two opens.

-**Deprecating `(mode promote)`** ([ocaml/dune#14044](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14044)): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed deprecating promote rule mode, arguing both use cases are better served by `diff` actions and a planned `--promote-file` flag.
ecosystem: -**Source archive disappearance** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29646](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29646)): [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) reported `exenum.0.84` and its upstream repository are missing (404), with comments also noting missing `azure-cosmos-db.0.1.3` and `omtl` packages.
oxcaml: -**Optimized Patricia trees** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627)): detailed debate between [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) on specialising `Set`/`Map` vs. fixing compiler function specialisation, with benchmarks showing a ~5.7% full-repo speedup; [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770) as an alternative using callback modules.

-**GADTs with staging review** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641)): detailed review from [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) covering closure allocations, exception handling in environment traversals, and fragility of stage tracking in error messages; a ticket was created to track accurate stage information in error traces.
multicore: -**`Eio.Path.load` correctness for virtual filesystems**: [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) reported in [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) that `Path.load` uses file size as an exact buffer bound causing silent truncation and empty results for `/proc` files; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) acknowledged overlap with [ocaml-multicore/eio#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798).

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Staging and metaprogramming system maturing rapidly**: GADTs, local constraints, sound moding, and jkind assertions all landed in a single week ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674)), forming a coherent prototype; `Tquote`/`Tquote_eval` type forms are now appearing across Merlin analysis modules ([oxcaml/merlin#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode system expansion**: `write`/`writing` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)) and `corrupted`/`corruptible` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)) modes fill gaps in mode implications; a full mode polymorphism system is in draft in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5478](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5478).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Backend codegen improvements accumulating**: X86 peephole optimizations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), improved int comparison lowering ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738)), CFG duplicate comparisons ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5740](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5740)), prologue shrink-wrap ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5762](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5762)), and constant propagation in CFG ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5753](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5753)) are all in flight concurrently.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Multicore-specific correctness issues** being worked through concurrently: continuation memory leaks ([ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580)), cleanup-at-exit races ([ocaml/ocaml#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14642)), and ephemeron GC liveness ([ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594)) all active.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Bytecode backend attention**: multiple fixes landed in the same week ([ocaml/ocaml#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14534), [ocaml/ocaml#14641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14641), [ocaml/ocaml#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13588)), reflecting increased attention to bytecode correctness and its use by js_of_ocaml.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Windows parity in dune**: hardlink sandboxing enabled ([ocaml/dune#13987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13987)), line-ending CI check added ([ocaml/dune#14013](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14013)), and MSYS2 depext improvements ([ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29579)) in progress.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin indexer scalability**: two concurrent PRs ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050), [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) target `ocaml-index` from complementary directions — bounded memory via LRU eviction and reduced write volume via pointer-based index sharing.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm backend advancement in js_of_ocaml**: three correctness fixes merged ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2210](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2210)) alongside large in-progress PRs for Dynlink/toplevel ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) and OCaml 5.5 support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Formal methods and SMT solvers** had a concentrated week: binsec 0.11.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29630](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29630)), bitwuzla 1.0.6 with quantifiers ([ocaml/opam-repository#29624](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29624)), smtml 0.24.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29616](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29616)), and ARM's ASL reference implementation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29635](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29635)) all landed.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Observability maturity**: opentelemetry 0.90 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29633](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29633)) split signal providers into separate trace, metrics, and log packages, a sign of the OCaml telemetry stack stabilising around well-defined interfaces.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Long-stalled RFCs being revived via structured meetings**: [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) (implicit source positions) reactivated after approximately a year with a proposal for a caml-devel meeting, following the pattern of resolving design deadlocks through structured synchronous discussion rather than async PR threads.
